summary: Velari full-stack smoke + regression. 19/19 pytest backend tests pass. Frontend smoke (Landing, Pricing, Signup → Onboarding → Today, Tasks, Calendar, Focus countdown, Weekly Review stepper, Login) all pass. Fixed one critical bug: /api/auth/register was returning 500 due to MongoDB ObjectId (_id) being injected into the response dict by Motor after insert_one (response excluded password_hash but not _id).

minor.issue: GET start/end filter uses ambiguous boolean precedence (no parens around `or`); start/end query may return unintended rows. Function: list_events. Not user-blocking yet because frontend fetches all events without start/end.

CORSMiddleware is hardcoded with allow_origins=['*'] and ignores the CORS_ORIGINS env logic that was computed above it (origin_list/allow_credentials are dead code). Works for now because frontend uses Bearer tokens, but cookie-only auth from other origins will fail.

action_items: (Already fixed by testing agent) /api/auth/register 500 — MongoDB _id leaked into JSON response. Fixed by excluding _id alongside password_hash.
Optional: tighten CORS — either honor the CORS_ORIGINS env logic that's currently dead code, or remove it for clarity.
Optional: fix /api/events start/end filter boolean precedence (use parens) and validate datetime range comparisons against ISO strings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 850 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, tasks, habits, events, focus, reviews, ai) for maintainability.
Register endpoint previously returned the raw Mongo insert dict (which Motor mutates to include ObjectId _id). Always project _id away or rebuild the response dict.
CORS block has dead branch: middleware uses literal ['*'] / allow_credentials=False, ignoring the env-derived origin_list. Either wire it up or remove.

list_events uses `start <= e['start'] <= end or start <= e['end'] <= end` without parens — relies on Python precedence which happens to work, but is fragile; also string comparison on ISO dates is correct only if both sides are same format/timezone.
Quick-capture AI parse swallows all exceptions silently and falls back to raw text — fine, but log level is `warning` with no metric; consider returning a flag so UI can surface 'parsed without AI'.
All AI endpoints construct a new LlmChat per call; fine for stateless prompts, but session_id is regenerated, so no conversation memory for /ai/chat — confirm this is intentional.
test_credentials: Admin: founder@velari.app / VelariAdmin2026! — works. New signup users via random email work (avoid .test TLD; pydantic EmailStr rejects reserved TLDs).
seed_data_creation: Admin user seeded by backend startup. Tests create + clean up TEST_ prefixed tasks/habits/events. A handful of TEST_ reviews and a TEST_focus session remain in admin account (intentional retention for /reviews and /focus/today).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers health, auth (register/login/me/logout/invalid), onboarding, tasks CRUD + quick-capture, habits flow, events, focus start/complete/today, reviews + summary, and all 4 AI endpoints. Frontend smoke uses data-testids defined in the task spec; Focus timer was verified to decrement from 24:59 → 24:53 over 5s. Weekly review stepper (wins → challenges → reflection w/ AI insight → next week → seal) verified end-to-end. Do NOT use .test TLD for fixture emails (EmailStr rejects it).
rca of the issue: BUG: POST /api/auth/register returned 500 Internal Server Error. RCA: AsyncIOMotorClient's insert_one mutates the passed dict in-place by setting the autogenerated ObjectId on key `_id`. The register handler returned that mutated dict (filtering only password_hash). FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize ObjectId → raises ValueError → 500. Mitigation: also strip `_id` from the response. The /auth/login handler already did `user.pop('_id', None)` — the same pattern is now applied to /auth/register.
